mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter expansion rate) drives positive equity valuations through increased corporate earnings expectations. A sustained quarterly growth rate above 2.5% annualized signals robust demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24180.419 reflects post-pandemic recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth quarters produce negative 20-day forward returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism activates when FEDFUNDS crosses above the 4.5% threshold and remains active until rate cuts resume.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.63% is below restrictive threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% shows stable labor market
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes > 0.3pp in 3 months but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 during unemployment shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) month-over-month growth rate interacts with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth to signal real economic capacity expansion. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive momentum simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved supply-side productivity and labor availability, producing positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.6475 and PAYEMS at 159001 both near recent highs
failure_condition: Simultaneous positive INDPRO and PAYEMS growth produces negative 20-day returns in > 48% of instances, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month growth rate declines by more than 1.5% from the prior month's growth rate, signaling weakening consumer demand. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market participants reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth rate declines > 1.5pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 757036.0 million dollars reflects consumer spending levels
failure_condition: Retail sales growth deceleration > 1.5pp produces negative 5-day returns in < 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth rate above 3.5% combined with FEDFUNDS below 3.0% creates a negative real interest rate regime. This regime signals monetary accommodation despite inflation, reducing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 333.979 and FEDFUNDS at 3.63% define current monetary-inflation regime
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Negative real rate regime produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 15% from the prior month signals deteriorating residential investment demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This housing shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction employment and materials demand contract.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1392.0 thousand units shows recent revision from 1465.0
failure_condition: Housing starts decline > 15% produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 during housing shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) month-over-month growth rate combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) stability (change < 0.1pp) signals healthy labor market momentum. This dual signal of job creation without rising joblessness produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved consumer confidence and earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159001 and UNRATE at 4.3% reflect stable labor market conditions
failure_condition: Simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability produce negative 20-day returns in > 48% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.
